text.title: langchain-chains Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Using `RunnableParallel` for batch processing expecting concurrency.
antipattern.reason: Parallel dict keys execute synchronously in CPython; total time = sum of branches.

section.p: Symptom: batch latency = sum, not max. Root cause: misconception. Fix: switch to `asyncio.gather` over `ainvoke` per element.

antipattern.description: `with_fallbacks()` called without `exceptions_to_handle`; catches every exception including auth and 4xx.
antipattern.reason: Auth and validation errors silently route to backup providers; the bug is invisible in logs.

section.p: Symptom: every call goes through fallback path; primary "looks" broken when it's a config bug. Root cause: unscoped exception handling. Fix: set `exceptions_to_handle=(RateLimitError, APITimeoutError, ...)`; let other exceptions propagate.

antipattern.description: Piping `JsonOutputParser` into a streaming chain and consuming partial dicts.
antipattern.reason: `JsonOutputParser` returns partial JSON during streaming; downstream code that expects complete dicts crashes.

section.p: Symptom: KeyError on partial chunks. Root cause: streaming + JsonOutputParser. Fix: switch to `with_structured_output` (does not stream partial), OR wait for the full message before parsing.

antipattern.description: `RunnableBranch` without a default chain; unmatched inputs raise an error.
antipattern.reason: Production traffic includes inputs not covered by the conditions; pipeline crashes instead of degrading.

section.p: Symptom: production NoMatch errors. Root cause: missing default. Fix: add a `general_chain` as the last argument to `RunnableBranch`; never let routing fall off the end.
